hindiTranslation: अध्यात्मज्ञानमें नित्यनिरन्तर रहना, तत्त्वज्ञानके अर्थरूप परमात्माको सब जगह देखना  यह पूर्वोक्त साधनसमुदाय तो ज्ञान है और जो इसके विपरीत है वह अज्ञान है  ऐसा कहा गया है ॥
englishTranslation: Constant awareness of the inner reality, and seeing the supreme reality as the meaning of true understanding—this is called knowledge; whatever is contrary to this is ignorance.
essence: Knowledge is seeing reality steadily; everything else is confusion.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Real knowledge means staying aware of the inner reality and seeing the supreme reality everywhere. Anything that misses this is ignorance.
simpleInsight: Clear seeing feels simpler than grasping at appearances.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna is defining what real understanding looks like. After listing the qualities of a mature seeker, he says knowledge is steady awareness of the inner reality and seeing the supreme reality everywhere; anything else is ignorance.
modernRelevance: You keep chasing labels, opinions, and surface facts in a tense conversation, but the deeper issue is what is actually real. This verse pulls attention back to that.
translationLiteral: अध्यात्म-ज्ञान (knowledge of the inner reality) / नित्यत्वम् (constantness) / तत्त्व-ज्ञान-अर्थ-दर्शनम् (seeing the meaning of knowledge of reality) / एतत् (this) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / इति (thus) / प्रोक्तम् (is called) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / यत् (which) / अतः अन्यथा (other than this)
